steps.title: Test statement one
steps.content: Statement I says angles $B$ and $C$ are equal within triangle $ABC$. That does not give matching information about triangle $DEF$, so it cannot prove the two triangles are congruent.

steps.title: Use the included side
steps.content:
Statement II gives $AC=DF$. Together with 
$$
AB=DE=7
$$ and angles $A$ and $D$ both measuring $50^\circ$, the equal angle is included between the two equal sides. That proves congruence by SAS.

steps.title: Reject the side case
steps.content: Statement III gives $BC=EF$, which creates the SSA situation with the given side and angle. SSA can produce two different triangles, so it is not sufficient to prove congruence.

steps.title: Select the statement
steps.content: Only statement II is sufficient, while statements I and III are not. The answer is B.
